channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kenya to Streamline Healthcare Access for Teachers Through SHA Following KNUT Talks
item.description: The Kenyan government is stepping up efforts to make healthcare more accessible for teachers by transitioning them to the Social Health Authority (SHA). Health CS Aden Duale met with Kenya National Un...

item.title: US Strikes Iranian Military Targets on Kharg Island Amid Escalating Regional Conflict
item.description: The US has carried out military strikes on Iran’s Kharg Island, a strategic hub handling about 90% of the country’s crude oil exports, targeting over 90 military facilities while leaving oil infrastru...

item.title: Author Sarah Perry Reflects on Life, Writing, and Personal Values
item.description: Sarah Perry, the acclaimed British author, spoke candidly in an interview about her personal life, literary career, and reflections on human nature. Born in Essex, Perry pursued English at Anglia Poly...

item.title: Investor Recovers Full MFT Investment Through Legal Documentation and Law Enforcement Support
item.description: An anonymous investor shared their experience recovering funds invested in the MFT Group of Companies, a private equity firm alleged to have operated a Ponzi-like scheme and faced multiple legal charg...

item.title: Early AFL action: Swans dominate Lions, Cats stage comeback, Pies struggle against Crows
item.description: The AFL season is off to a thrilling start with several key matches making headlines. The Sydney Swans have hit the ground running against the Brisbane Lions, taking control early thanks to rapid ball...

item.title: Minister Makonda Calls for Accountability in Sports Associations
item.description: In Dar es Salaam, the Minister for Information, Culture, Arts and Sports, Paul Makonda, has directed all registered sports associations and federations to undergo audits and submit full financial repo...

item.title: Pakistan Airports Authority Advises Airlines to Minimise Jet Fuel Uplift Amid Regional Supply Disruptions
item.description: The Pakistan Airports Authority (PAA) has issued a Notice to Airmen (Notam) directing all airlines operating flights from Pakistan to depart with only the minimum required fuel. This precautionary mea...

item.title: Musk’s xAI Undergoes Major Reorganization Amid Competitive Pressures
item.description: Elon Musk’s AI company, xAI, is undergoing another major restructuring three years after its founding. Of the original 11 co-founders, only two remain, as the company struggles to compete with rivals ...

item.title: Trump Shares Surveillance Footage Alleging Strikes on Iran's Kharg Island
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump released a monochrome video on Friday which he claims shows multiple targets on Iran's Kharg Island being struck. The footage, described as surveillance imagery, appears to...

item.title: Investigation launched after man’s body discovered in wheelie bin in Coventry park
item.description: Police in Coventry are investigating after the body of a man, believed to be between 40 and 50 years old, was discovered in a wheelie bin at Cash's Park off Daimler Road. Authorities suspect that the ...

item.title: Bhutan Struggles to Expand Crypto Payments Despite Large Bitcoin Reserves
item.description: Bhutan introduced a nationwide crypto payment network last May, allowing tourists to pay for visas, flights, hotels, and meals in over 100 cryptocurrencies through Binance. Initially, over 1,000 merch...

item.title: Weekly National and Global Updates: Marcos at UN, Zaldy Co Case, and Middle East Crisis
item.description: This week’s news highlights major developments both in the Philippines and globally. President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. is making another push for a Philippine seat on the United Nations Security Council,...

item.title: Bloemfontein pays tribute to anti-apartheid veteran Mosiuoa Lekota
item.description: Hundreds of mourners gathered in Bloemfontein to pay their respects to Mosiuoa Lekota, a respected political leader and anti-apartheid activist, who will be accorded a Special Official Funeral Categor...

item.title: Legendary Super Eagles Midfielder Henry Nwosu Passes Away at 62
item.description: Dem wahala don come for Nigerian football as Henry Nwosu, one former Super Eagles midfielder, don die at 62. The football legend wey hail from Imo State, don pass away for Lagos State University Teach...

item.title: Temporary suspension of flight operations at Islamabad International Airport on 16 March due to operational reasons
item.description: The Pakistan Airports Authority (PAA) announced that flight operations at Islamabad International Airport will be temporarily suspended on 16 March 2026 from 8:30am to 10:30am Pakistan Standard Time. ...

item.title: 20 Important Facts About Henry Nwosu, Nigerian Football Legend
item.description: The Nigerian football community dey mourn the loss of Henry Onyemanze Nwosu, one of the most talented midfielders wey Nigeria don ever get. Born on June 14, 1963, for Imo State, Nwosu rise to fame dur...

item.title: Nigerian Army stops armed attack while responding to communal dispute in Cross River
item.description: On Saturday, troops from the 13 Brigade of the Nigerian Army successfully defended themselves against a coordinated ambush by suspected armed criminals while responding to a communal dispute in Odonge...

item.title: Salman Ali Agha Receives ICC Reprimand After Bangladesh ODI Incident
item.description: Pakistan cricket captain Salman Ali Agha was officially reprimanded by the International Cricket Council (ICC) for breaching Level 1 of the Code of Conduct during the second ODI against Bangladesh in ...

item.title: VP Sara Duterte expresses concern over House request to take Madriaga into custody
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte has expressed being disturbed by a recent request from Akbayan Representative Chel Diokno for the House of Representatives to place Ramil Madriaga under its custody. The ma...

item.title: Why Australian Farmers Purchase Large Diesel Stocks to Protect Food Supply
item.description: Over recent weeks, rising petrol and diesel prices have sparked concerns across Australia about potential impacts on food prices. While urban motorists feel the pinch, farmers in regional areas often ...
